<!-- Generated by scripts/sync_chebfun_examples.py. -->
<!-- Source: https://www.chebfun.org/examples/ode-linear/WikiODE.html -->

<div class="chebfun-import chebfun-example-import">
<div class='page-header'>
<h1>Wikipedia ODE examples</h1>
<h2>Mark Richardson, September 2010 in <a href='../'>ode-linear</a><span><a href='/examples/ode-linear/WikiODE.m'>download</a><span class='sep-sm'>&middot;</span><a href='//github.com/chebfun/examples/blob/master/ode-linear/WikiODE.m'>view on GitHub</a></span></h2>
</div>

<div id='content' class="col-sm-12 example" role="main">
<p>Here, we solve three simple linear problems considered in the Wikipedia article on ODEs [1]. The problems are solved in the order they appear in the article, with boundary conditions imposed to make the solutions unique.</p>
<h3 id="problem-1-second-order-problem">Problem 1: Second-order problem</h3>
<p>$$ L(y) = y'' - 4y' + 5y = 0, \quad
y(-1) = e^{-2} \cos(-1) , ~~ y(1) = e^2\cos(1).  $$</p>
<p>Begin by defining the domain $d$, chebfun variable $x$ and operator $N$.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">d = [-1 1];
x = chebfun('x',d);
N = chebop(d);</pre>

<p>The problem has Dirichlet boundary conditions.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">N.lbc = exp(-2)*cos(-1);
N.rbc = exp(2)*cos(1);</pre>

<p>Define the linear operator.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">N.op = @(y) diff(y,2) - 4*diff(y,1) + 5*y;</pre>

<p>Define the right-hand side of the ODE.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">rhs = 0;</pre>

<p>Solve the ODE using backslash.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">y = N\rhs;</pre>

<p>Analytic solution.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">y_exact = exp(2*x)*cos(x);</pre>

<p>How close is the computed solution to the true solution?</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">norm(y-y_exact)</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">ans =
     1.393596354406182e-13
</pre>

<p>Plot the computed solution.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">plot(y), grid on</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/ode-linear/WikiODE_01.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<h3 id="problem-2-simple-harmonic-oscillator">Problem 2: Simple harmonic oscillator</h3>
<p>$$ L(y) = y'' + \pi^2 y = 0,  \qquad y(-1) = -1, ~~ y'(1) = -\pi. $$</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">d = [-1 1];
x = chebfun('x',d);
N = chebop(d);
N.op = @(y) diff(y,2) + pi^2*y;</pre>

<p>This problem has a Dirichlet boundary condition on the left,</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">N.lbc = -1;</pre>

<p>and a Neumann condition on the right.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">N.rbc = @(u) diff(u) + pi;</pre>

<p>Define the right-hand side of the ODE.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">rhs = 0;</pre>

<p>Solve the ODE using backslash.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">y = N\rhs;</pre>

<p>Analytic solution.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">y_exact = cos(pi*x)+sin(pi*x);</pre>

<p>How close is the computed solution to the true solution?</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">norm(y-y_exact)</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">ans =
     2.470505881658372e-14
</pre>

<p>Plot the computed solution.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">plot(y), grid on</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/ode-linear/WikiODE_02.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<h3 id="problem-3-first-order-problem">Problem 3: First-order problem</h3>
<p>$$ L(y) = y' + 3y = 2 \qquad y(0) = 2 . $$</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">d = [0 1];
x = chebfun('x',d);
N = chebop(d);</pre>

<p>First-order problems require only one boundary condition.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">N.lbc = 2;</pre>

<p>Define the linear operator.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">N.op = @(y) diff(y) + 3*y - 2;</pre>

<p>Define the right-hand side of the ODE.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">rhs = 0;</pre>

<p>Solve the ODE using backslash.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">y = N\rhs;</pre>

<p>Analytic solution, usually found with integrating factors.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">y_exact = 2/3 + 4/3*exp(-3*x);</pre>

<p>How close is the computed solution to the true solution?</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">norm(y-y_exact)</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">ans =
     2.987830876459629e-12
</pre>

<p>Plot the computed solution</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">plot(y), grid on</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/ode-linear/WikiODE_03.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<h3 id="references">References</h3>
<ol>
<li><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linear_differential_equation">http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linear_differential_equation</a>.</li>
</ol></div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
</div>
